## Summary
<!-- What changed and why? Keep this concise and reviewer-oriented. -->

## Traceability

<!--
Complete this before opening the PR.

For AIS Spec framework changes, use GitHub issue IDs as the spec tracking
record (for example, #142). For downstream project repos, use the repo-local
spec IDs or task references that project uses. Do not list every task ID when a
short coverage summary is clearer.
-->

- Tracking issue/spec: <!-- e.g., #142 for AIS Spec, or 2602-001 in project repos -->
- Task coverage: <!-- e.g., linked issue covers the work, representative task IDs, or N/A with rationale -->
- Lifecycle phase: <!-- specify | design | tasks | implement | maintain | docs/framework -->
- Traceability notes: <!-- What should reviewers know about scope, task coverage, or intentional exceptions? -->

## Release note
<!-- Required. This text is used in CHANGELOG.md and the GitHub Release. -->
<!-- Add exactly one release label on the PR: release:patch, release:minor, or release:major. -->
<!-- For release:major, include a line starting with: BREAKING CHANGE: -->

## Validation evidence
<!-- List the commands, checks, or manual review performed before opening the PR. -->

- <!-- e.g., Spec frontmatter status reviewed; complete specs have all tasks checked off -->
- <!-- e.g., bash .specify/scripts/bash/generate-commands.sh --check -->
- <!-- e.g., npx markdownlint-cli2 "**/*.md" -->
- <!-- e.g., Manual review: PR template renders correctly and release note is complete -->
